devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । निशम्य गदतामेवं ऋषीणां धनुषि ध्रुवः । सन्दधेऽस्त्रमुपस्पृश्य यन्नारायणनिर्मितम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaniśamya gadatāmevaṃ ṛṣīṇāṃ dhanuṣi dhruvaḥsandadhe'stramupaspṛśya yannārāyaṇanirmitam
translation:
Maitreya said:
Hearing this speech of the sages, Dhruva sipped some water as ācamana, and set to his bow the missile created by Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: सन्धीयमान एतस्मिन् माया गुह्यकनिर्मिताः । क्षिप्रं विनेशुर्विदुर क्लेशा ज्ञानोदये यथा ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sandhīyamāna etasmin māyā guhyakanirmitāḥkṣipraṃ vineśurvidura kleśā jñānodaye yathā
translation: Oh Vidura, just as afflictions disappear at the dawn of the (highest) knowledge, the manifestations of the black magic created by Guhyakas, immediately vanished, while the missile was being set on the bow.

devanagari: तस्यार्षास्त्रं धनुषि प्रयुञ्जतः । सुवर्णपुङ्खाः कलहंसवाससः । विनिःसृता आविविशुर्द्विषद्‍बलं । यथा वनं भीमरवाः शिखण्डिनः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyārṣāstraṃ dhanuṣi prayuñjataḥsuvarṇapuṅkhāḥ kalahaṃsavāsasaḥviniḥsṛtā āviviśurdviṣad‍balaṃyathā vanaṃ bhīmaravāḥ śikhaṇḍinaḥ
translation: Even while he was fixing on his bow the missile devised by the sage Nārāyaṇa, shafts with gold-plated heads and feathers resembling the wings of swans, shot forth from it, and penetrated the enemy’s forces like peacocks entering a forest, with a terrible cry.

devanagari: तैस्तिग्मधारैः प्रधने शिलीमुखैः । इतस्ततः पुण्यजना उपद्रुताः । तमभ्यधावन् कुपिता उदायुधाः । सुपर्णं उन्नद्धफणा इवाहयः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: taistigmadhāraiḥ pradhane śilīmukhaiḥitastataḥ puṇyajanā upadrutāḥtamabhyadhāvan kupitā udāyudhāḥsuparṇaṃ unnaddhaphaṇā ivāhayaḥ
translation: Assailed on every side, in the battle, by those sharp-pointed arrows, the Yakṣas (Puṇyajanas) got enraged. With their weapons raised, they rushed towards him on all sides, just as cobras with their hoods raised, would attack Garuḍa.

devanagari: स तान् पृषत्कैरभिधावतो मृधे । निकृत्तबाहूरुशिरोधरोदरान् । निनाय लोकं परमर्कमण्डलं । व्रजन्ति निर्भिद्य यमूर्ध्वरेतसः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tān pṛṣatkairabhidhāvato mṛdhenikṛttabāhūruśirodharodarānnināya lokaṃ paramarkamaṇḍalaṃvrajanti nirbhidya yamūrdhvaretasaḥ
translation: Even as they were attacking him in the battle, he cut down their arms, thighs, necks and stomachs, and sent them to that highest region, to which sages with lifelong celibacy attain, penetrating their way through the sphere of the Sun.

devanagari: तान् हन्यमानानभिवीक्ष्य गुह्यकान् । अनागसश्चित्ररथेन भूरिशः । औत्तानपादिं कृपया पितामहो । मनुर्जगादोपगतः सहर्षिभिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tān hanyamānānabhivīkṣya guhyakānanāgasaścitrarathena bhūriśaḥauttānapādiṃ kṛpayā pitāmahomanurjagādopagataḥ saharṣibhiḥ
translation: His grandfather Manu saw that the innocent Yakṣas were being slaughtered in great numbers by Dhruva, of a wonderful chariot. Out of compassion, he, accompanied by sages, approached Dhruva, the son of Uttānapāda and spoke thus:

devanagari: मनुरुवाच । अलं वत्सातिरोषेण तमोद्वारेण पाप्मना । येन पुण्यजनानेतान् अवधीस्त्वं अनागसः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: manuruvācaalaṃ vatsātiroṣeṇa tamodvāreṇa pāpmanāyena puṇyajanānetān avadhīstvaṃ anāgasaḥ
translation:
Manu said:
Oh child, enough of this sinful rage which is a gateway to Tama (hell)—rage, (blinded) with which you have killed these innocent Yakṣas.

devanagari: नास्मत्कुलोचितं तात कर्मैतत् सद्विगर्हितम् । वधो यदुपदेवानां आरब्धस्तेऽकृतैनसाम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsmatkulocitaṃ tāta karmaitat sadvigarhitamvadho yadupadevānāṃ ārabdhaste'kṛtainasām
translation: Oh child I This act of slaughtering innocent demigods (Yakṣas) commenced by you, is censured by the good. It is not becoming to our race.

devanagari: नन्वेकस्यापराधेन प्रसङ्गाद् बहवो हताः । भ्रातुर्वधाभितप्तेन त्वयाङ्ग भ्रातृवत्सल ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nanvekasyāparādhena prasaṅgād bahavo hatāḥbhrāturvadhābhitaptena tvayāṅga bhrātṛvatsala
translation: Being enraged at the murder of your brother, whom you dearly loved, a great number (of Yakṣas) have been killed by you, for the crime of one (Yakṣa), on your suspicion of their involvement in it.

devanagari: नायं मार्गो हि साधूनां हृषीकेशानुवर्तिनाम् । यदात्मानं पराग्गृह्य पशुवद्भूतवैशसम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nāyaṃ mārgo hi sādhūnāṃ hṛṣīkeśānuvartināmyadātmānaṃ parāggṛhya paśuvadbhūtavaiśasam
translation: To massacre creatures like beasts, mistaking the gross body for the Soul (ātman)—this is not the way of the righteous who are devoted to Hari (the Lord of senśe-organs).

devanagari: सर्वभूतात्मभावेन भूतावासं हरिं भवान् । आराध्याप दुराराध्यं विष्णोस्तत्परमं पदम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvabhūtātmabhāvena bhūtāvāsaṃ hariṃ bhavānārādhyāpa durārādhyaṃ viṣṇostatparamaṃ padam
translation: Having propitiated Hari who dwells within all creatures[1], and who is difficult to be pleased, and looking upon all living beings as (a part of) your own self,[2] you have secured for yourself the highest realm belonging to Lord Viṣṇu.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Or: who is the abode of all creatures.

Footnote [2]:
Sarva-bhūtātma-bhāvena: (i) Looking upon Hari as the indwelling Soul of all creatures.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Hari as antaryāmin of all creatures abides in all beings.—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Identifying yourself with all creatures.—VC.
(iv) Realizing that all creatures, mobile and immobile are your own Self.—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: स त्वं हरेरनुध्यातः तत्पुंसामपि सम्मतः । कथं त्ववद्यं कृतवान् अनुशिक्षन्सतां व्रतम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ hareranudhyātaḥ tatpuṃsāmapi sammataḥkathaṃ tvavadyaṃ kṛtavān anuśikṣansatāṃ vratam
translation: You are constantly remembered by Hari, and are highly respected by his devotees. How is it that you, who should instruct others by your (exemplary) righteous conduct, have committed this censurable act?

devanagari: तितिक्षया करुणया मैत्र्या चाखिलजन्तुषु । समत्वेन च सर्वात्मा भगवान् सम्प्रसीदति ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: titikṣayā karuṇayā maitryā cākhilajantuṣusamatvena ca sarvātmā bhagavān samprasīdati
translation: It is by patience, compassion and friendliness[3] and the attitude of equality[4] to all beings that the glorious Lord, who is the Soul (ātman) of all, becomes propitious.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bālaprabodhini thinks that the qualities are to be shown to the superiors, the inferiors and equals respectively. Thus patience to elders etc.

Footnote [4]:
samatvena—(i) To regard that all beings are essentially equal, as the Lord dwells within each of them.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) To show enmity to enemies and friendliness to friends is equality.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: सम्प्रसन्ने भगवति पुरुषः प्राकृतैर्गुणैः । विमुक्तो जीवनिर्मुक्तो ब्रह्म निर्वाणमृच्छति ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: samprasanne bhagavati puruṣaḥ prākṛtairguṇaiḥvimukto jīvanirmukto brahma nirvāṇamṛcchati
translation: When the Lord is thoroughly pleased, a man becomes free from all the guṇas of Prakṛti. Liberated from the stage of Jīva-hood (i.e. his subtle Liṅga Śarīra), he attains to the blissful Brahman.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
nirvāṇa (i) Characterised by the highest bliss.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) (Brahman) which is free from body or full of bliss—Padaratnāvalī VJ. strongly objects to the concept of merger with Brahman as Liberation (mukti) will be śūnya.
(iii) the highest (beyond atiśayas)—Siddhāntapradīpa
(iv) Beyond saṃsāra—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: भूतैः पञ्चभिरारब्धैः योषित्पुरुष एव हि । तयोर्व्यवायात् सम्भूतिः योषित्पुरुषयोरिह ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtaiḥ pañcabhirārabdhaiḥ yoṣitpuruṣa eva hitayorvyavāyāt sambhūtiḥ yoṣitpuruṣayoriha
translation: The male and the female are the products of the five gross elements developed in the forms of bodies. It is by their cohabitation that the creation of men and women takes place here in this world.

devanagari: एवं प्रवर्तते सर्गः स्थितिः संयम एव च । गुणव्यतिकराद् राजन् मायया परमात्मनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ pravartate sargaḥ sthitiḥ saṃyama eva caguṇavyatikarād rājan māyayā paramātmanaḥ
translation: Oh king, it is due to the intermingling of guṇas caused by the Māyā (power) of the Supreme Lord that the creation, preservation and destruction of the world, take place.

devanagari: निमित्तमात्रं तत्रासीत् निर्गुणः पुरुषर्षभः । व्यक्ताव्यक्तमिदं विश्वं यत्र भ्रमति लोहवत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: nimittamātraṃ tatrāsīt nirguṇaḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥvyaktāvyaktamidaṃ viśvaṃ yatra bhramati lohavat
translation: The Supreme Man who is devoid of all attributes, (like sattva, rajas etc.) is merely an apparent cause—therein that the universe consisting of manifested and unmanifested things, is in a state of flux like iron moved (by the presence of a magnet).

devanagari: स खल्विदं भगवान्कालशक्त्या । गुणप्रवाहेण विभक्तवीर्यः । करोत्यकर्तैव निहन्त्यहन्ता । चेष्टा विभूम्नः खलु दुर्विभाव्या ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa khalvidaṃ bhagavānkālaśaktyāguṇapravāheṇa vibhaktavīryaḥkarotyakartaiva nihantyahantāceṣṭā vibhūmnaḥ khalu durvibhāvyā
translation: [6] The Supreme Lord whose power and energy is distributed by the disturbance in the equilibrium of guṇas caused by his potency called Time, verily creates (the universe) without being its agent, and destroys it himself not being the destroyer. Inscrutable, indeed, is the working of the Almighty.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
The first half:
(i) The Lord’s power is differently distributed in different species of beings such as a man, a god etc., and according to the various degrees of happiness or unhappiness to be experienced according to their Karmas. His potency is so divided due to His power called Time, which causes, commotion among the guṇas.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) OR: For the sake of creating disturbance in the balance of guṇas for the creation etc. of the universe, he divides his power, i.e. He assumes the form of Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(iii) Padaratnāvalī accepts the v.l. yogaśaktyā for kāla-śaktyā.
It is by his power of Yoga that the Lord exhibits his power, and carries out the function of creation etc. through his three guṇas. Due to his effortlessness in creation he is regarded as non-creator. Though he destroys the universe, he has no enmity against anyone. Hence he is not regarded as destroyer.—Padaratnāvalī
The Lord is the cause of the creation etc. of the universe, but his workings defy our comprehension. The creation etc. of the world is due to the power of Time which creates commotion in guṇas.—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: सोऽनन्तोऽन्तकरः कालो ऽनादिरादिकृदव्ययः । जनं जनेन जनयन् मारयन् मृत्युनान्तकम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: so'nanto'ntakaraḥ kālo 'nādirādikṛdavyayaḥjanaṃ janena janayan mārayan mṛtyunāntakam
translation: [7] He is a creator, as he makes men beget men. Ashe destroys the destroyer (robbers etc.) by means of death (through kings and others), he is called the destroyer-—Time. But the Almighty Himself is endless (immortal), beginningless, unchangeable and subject to no dimunition.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Though he himself is endless (immortal), he brings about the end (of the world) as Time. Though he is without a beginning and imperishable, changeless, he brings about the beginning (of all). He is the creator by making mtn beget men and ḍestroys the destroyers (robbers etc.) through death.
(ii) Bhāgavata Candrikā points out that the Almighty whose form is Time, does not directly create or destroy but does so through the father or death.

devanagari: न वै स्वपक्षोऽस्य विपक्ष एव वा । परस्य मृत्योर्विशतः समं प्रजाः । तं धावमानं अनुधावन्त्यनीशा । यथा रजांस्यनिलं भूतसङ्घाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: na vai svapakṣo'sya vipakṣa eva vāparasya mṛtyorviśataḥ samaṃ prajāḥtaṃ dhāvamānaṃ anudhāvantyanīśāyathā rajāṃsyanilaṃ bhūtasaṅghāḥ
translation: [8] The Supreme Lord has nobody as his own or none as his enemy; (for) he enters all beings equally as (i.e. in the form of their) death. Not being their masters (due to their Karmas), all creatures follow[9] him in his course, just as particles of dust follow the blast of wind.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
(1) The Lord who is untouched by saṃsāra (or whose body is death), enters the bodies of all creatures as antaryāmin for controlling them, according to their karmas. Nobody is his friend or enemy. (All are equal to him). All creatures, not being their masters (but subject to their previous karmas) follow the Lord who runs (i.e. controls them according to their karmas) just as particles of dust helplessly follow the wind. (The dust particles may fall at any place or pit, the wind is impartial to all the particles).—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(2) Though God enters all beings as Death, creatures get different fruits according to their karmas despite God’s equality, just as the wind treats all particles of dust equally, though some of them fall in darkness, light, water or fire.—VC.

Footnote [9]:
anudhāvanti—(i) do the actions.—Siddhāntapradīpa
(ii) take births—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: आयुषोऽपचयं जन्तोः तथैवोपचयं विभुः । उभाभ्यां रहितः स्वस्थो दुःस्थस्य विदधात्यसौ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: āyuṣo'pacayaṃ jantoḥ tathaivopacayaṃ vibhuḥubhābhyāṃ rahitaḥ svastho duḥsthasya vidadhātyasau
translation: The All-pervading Lord cuts short or prolongs the life of a creature according to its destiny (previous karma), while he himself, being above these changes, abides in his natural state.

devanagari: केचित्कर्म वदन्त्येनं स्वभावमपरे नृप । एके कालं परे दैवं पुंसः काममुतापरे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kecitkarma vadantyenaṃ svabhāvamapare nṛpaeke kālaṃ pare daivaṃ puṃsaḥ kāmamutāpare
translation: Oh King (Dhruva)! Some call him Karma; others, Nature; some, Time-spirit; others, Destiny; while others, the Kāma (desire) of men.

devanagari: अव्यक्तस्याप्रमेयस्य नानाशक्त्युदयस्य च । न वै चिकीर्षितं तात को वेदाथ स्वसम्भवम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: avyaktasyāprameyasya nānāśaktyudayasya cana vai cikīrṣitaṃ tāta ko vedātha svasambhavam
translation: Verily nobody knows the design (purpose) of the Unmanifest Immeasurable Lord who is the source of innumerable potencies (like Mahat etc.). Oh child who can then know the Lord Who is one’s own origin?

devanagari: न चैते पुत्रक भ्रातुः हन्तारो धनदानुगाः । विसर्गादानयोस्तात पुंसो दैवं हि कारणम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: na caite putraka bhrātuḥ hantāro dhanadānugāḥvisargādānayostāta puṃso daivaṃ hi kāraṇam
translation: Oh dear son! These Yakṣas (the servants and followers of Kubera) are definitely not the slayers of your brother. For, oh child, it is the Destiny (daiva) alone, which brings about the birth or death of men.

devanagari: स एव विश्वं सृजति स एवावति हन्ति च । अथापि ह्यनहङ्कारात् नाज्यते गुणकर्मभिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva viśvaṃ sṛjati sa evāvati hanti caathāpi hyanahaṅkārāt nājyate guṇakarmabhiḥ
translation: It is he only who creates the universe. Nobody else but he alone protects and destroys it. In spite of this, he is not affected by guṇas (attributes) and karmas, as he is free from ahaṃkāra (ego).

devanagari: एष भूतानि भूतात्मा भूतेशो भूतभावनः । स्वशक्त्या मायया युक्तः सृजत्यत्ति च पाति च ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa bhūtāni bhūtātmā bhūteśo bhūtabhāvanaḥsvaśaktyā māyayā yuktaḥ sṛjatyatti ca pāti ca
translation: This Soul of created beings, their Lord and creator gets united with his Māyā power and creates, preserves and eats up (i.e. withdraws within him) the created beings.

devanagari: तमेव मृत्युममृतं तात दैवं । सर्वात्मनोपेहि जगत्परायणम् । यस्मै बलिं विश्वसृजो हरन्ति । गावो यथा वै नसि दामयन्त्रिताः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva mṛtyumamṛtaṃ tāta daivaṃsarvātmanopehi jagatparāyaṇamyasmai baliṃ viśvasṛjo harantigāvo yathā vai nasi dāmayantritāḥ
translation: Dear child! Completely submitting all your Self, seek the shelter of the Deity who is the final resort of the world, and who is the death itself (to the unrighteous, non-devotees)—Deity to whom creators of the universe bear offerings, just as bulls controlled by a string passed through their nostrils, carry the loads (of their) masters.

devanagari: यः पञ्चवर्षो जननीं त्वं विहाय । मातुः सपत्‍न्या वचसा भिन्नमर्मा । वनं गतस्तपसा प्रत्यगक्षं । आराध्य लेभे मूर्ध्नि पदं त्रिलोक्याः ॥ २८ ...
english_devnagari: yaḥ pañcavarṣo jananīṃ tvaṃ vihāyamātuḥ sapat‍nyā vacasā bhinnamarmāvanaṃ gatastapasā pratyagakṣaṃārādhya lebhe mūrdhni padaṃ trilokyāḥ
translation: Gut to the quick by the (harsh) words of your stepmother, you, as a child of five, went to the forest leaving back your mother. By your penance, you propitiated Lord Viṣṇu who is beyond the ken of senses, and secured (for you) a position at the head of the universe.

devanagari: तमेनमङ्गात्मनि मुक्तविग्रहे । व्यपाश्रितं निर्गुणमेकमक्षरम् । आत्मानमन्विच्छ विमुक्तमात्मदृग् । यस्मिन् इदं भेदमसत्प्रतीयते ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tamenamaṅgātmani muktavigrahevyapāśritaṃ nirguṇamekamakṣaramātmānamanviccha vimuktamātmadṛgyasmin idaṃ bhedamasatpratīyate
translation: Oh dear child, with your mind (lit. sight) turned inward (on your self),[10] seek the Supreme Lord who dwells in the heart that is free from enmity[11];—the Lord who is beyond attributes (guṇas); one (without a second), imperishable, eternally liberated and in whom this phenomenal world of differences, though unreal, appears to exist[12].
purport:
Footnote [10]:
ātmadṛk— Or one who looks upon all as himself.
(ii) With your mind concentrated on the Supreme Self.

Footnote [11]:
mukta-vigrahe—Adj. qualifying ātmani,
(i) the Soul which is indivisible (without parts).
(ii) Padaratnāvalī reads: vimukta-vigrahair vyapāśritam: Who is resorted to by Yogis who have become free from physical bodies.

Footnote [12]:
In the jīva, the worldly difference of being a god or a man is not part of his essence, but it is seen on account of meritorious or sinful karmas. It is not that way mithyā. This difference is unreal (asat), as it is always changing—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: त्वं प्रत्यगात्मनि तदा भगवत्यनन्त । आनन्दमात्र उपपन्नसमस्तशक्तौ । भक्तिं विधाय परमां शनकैरविद्या । ग्रन्थिं विभेत्स्यसि ममाहमिति प्ररूढ...
english_devnagari: tvaṃ pratyagātmani tadā bhagavatyanantaānandamātra upapannasamastaśaktaubhaktiṃ vidhāya paramāṃ śanakairavidyāgranthiṃ vibhetsyasi mamāhamiti prarūḍham
translation: (While seeking the Lord), You practise intense devotion to the Almighty Lord who abides in all as antaryāmin; who is infinite, pure and absolute bliss, and is possessed of all powers. Then you will gradually cut asunder the knot of avidyā (nescience) which has grown very strong by (brooding over the notions that this is) ‘I’ and ‘Mine’.

devanagari: संयच्छ रोषं भद्रं ते प्रतीपं श्रेयसां परम् । श्रुतेन भूयसा राजन् अगदेन यथामयम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃyaccha roṣaṃ bhadraṃ te pratīpaṃ śreyasāṃ paramśrutena bhūyasā rājan agadena yathāmayam
translation: Oh King, by your deep and extensive study of the śāstras, control your anger which is a formidable hindrance to attaining to the final beatitude, just as you would control a disease with a medicine. May God bless you!

devanagari: येनोपसृष्टात्पुरुषात् लोक उद्विजते भृशम् । न बुधस्तद्वशं गच्छेद् इच्छन् अभयमात्मनः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yenopasṛṣṭātpuruṣāt loka udvijate bhṛśamna budhastadvaśaṃ gacched icchan abhayamātmanaḥ
translation: A wise man who wishes to attain the state of fearlessness for himself, should not allow himself to be overpowered by that passion (anger). For people get afraid of a person who is overwhelmed with anger.

devanagari: हेलनं गिरिशभ्रातुः धनदस्य त्वया कृतम् । यज्जघ्निवान् पुण्यजनान् भ्रातृघ्नानित्यमर्षितः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: helanaṃ giriśabhrātuḥ dhanadasya tvayā kṛtamyajjaghnivān puṇyajanān bhrātṛghnānityamarṣitaḥ
translation: Inasmuch as you, being enraged, have killed Yakṣas thinking that they were the slayers of your brother, you have committed a grave offence against Kubera, the brother (i.e. friend) of god Śiva (the Lord of the mountains).

devanagari: तं प्रसादय वत्साशु सन्नत्या प्रश्रयोक्तिभिः । न यावन्महतां तेजः कुलं नोऽभिभविष्यति ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ prasādaya vatsāśu sannatyā praśrayoktibhiḥna yāvanmahatāṃ tejaḥ kulaṃ no'bhibhaviṣyati
translation: Oh child, while the wrath of the exalted Souls (like Kubera) does not vanquish our race, you try immediately to propitiate him by paying him obeisance, and offering humble apologies.

devanagari: एवं स्वायम्भुवः पौत्रं अनुशास्य मनुर्ध्रुवम् । तेनाभिवन्दितः साकं ऋषिभिः स्वपुरं ययौ ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ svāyambhuvaḥ pautraṃ anuśāsya manurdhruvamtenābhivanditaḥ sākaṃ ṛṣibhiḥ svapuraṃ yayau
translation:
Having thus admonished his grandson Dhruva, who bowed down to him, Svāyambhuva Manu returned to his city along with the sages.
[back to top]
